articles.title: A Space Explosion That's Like Nothing We've Seen Before!
articles.summary: Imagine a supernova and a neutron star crash happening at the same time! That's what scientists think they saw. The event, called AT2025ulz, started like a kilonova (a gold-making explosion) but then turned into a supernova. If it's real, it's a brand-new type of cosmic blast called a 'superkilonova.'

articles.title: Meet LUCA: The Great-Great-Great-Great… Grandparent of Every Living Thing!
articles.summary: Scientists are getting closer to understanding LUCA, the Last Universal Common Ancestor. LUCA was a single cell that lived billions of years ago, and every living thing today — from humans to bacteria to trees — is related to it. Researchers now think LUCA was a rod-shaped microbe that lived near a deep-sea vent.

articles.title: This Teacher Built a Giant 1940s Computer With His Students — And It's Awesome!
articles.summary: Tom Burick, a tech teacher at a school for students with autism, led his class in building a life-size replica of ENIAC, one of the world's first electronic computers. The original ENIAC was built in 1946 and was 1,000 times faster than other machines. Burick, who has dyscalculia, says his own learning differences helped him become a better engineer and teacher.
